message: parseCodexACPUsageMeta accepts any non-empty total_token_usage object as valid; unknown-only or missing-field objects unmarshal to zero counts and return Captured=true instead of treating malformed metadata as a miss.
ACP runs do not emit the required no-usage warning when Codex ACP metadata is absent or malformed.

The malformed metadata tests do not cover a non-empty total_token_usage object with missing/wrong token fields; the parser only checks that the object has at least one field and then unmarshals absent int fields as zero, so malformed metadata such as {"foo":1} is captured as real zero usage instead of preserving captured=false.
